title: ulistnew
text: ulistnew Create an item list. Explain this code long ulistnew (UHACThAny, UHACT*hList); Copy Parameters hAny Any valid handle hList Handle to the new item list (Output) Return Values Table 1. Values returned by ulistnew Value Meaning 0 Success. The list was created. -165 The supplied handle is not valid. -1406 Memory could not be allocated or freed. Use Library: ucall Description ulistnew creates a new item list in which items can be inserted using ulistput . This list can then be passed to a 4GL operation, as a STRING parameter, and the operation can access the list using the ProcScript functions getitem and/or getlistitems . The parameter hAny can be any valid handle you have in your program (environment handle, instance handle, and so forth). When you are done with a list, you must free it by calling ufreeh . Example: ulistnew, ulist2uf, ulistdel, and ulistput The following example creates an associative list, consisting of country codes and names, and passes it to a service, which prints it to the message frame. The operation DOLIST in service TESTSVC is defined as follows: Explain this code operation dolist params string thelist : in; endparams putmess "%%thelist" end Copy The 3GL code that creates the list and passes it to service DOLIST is as follows: Explain this code char *countries[] = { "NL", "Netherlands", "GB", "Great Britain", "D", "Germany", "F", "France", "?", "huh?", "I", "Italy", "E", "Spain", 0 }; #include <h3gl.h> void pass_itemlist () { UHACT hEnv, hIn; /* Environment and instance handle */ UHACT hOp; /* Operation handles for OP1 amd OP2 */ long actStatus, procErr; /* Activation status and procerr */ UHACT hList; /* Item list handle */ int i; /* Loop variable */ ueopen(&hEnv); /* Get environment handle */ uinstnew (hEnv, (UCHAR *)"TESTSVC", 0, 0, UDEFAULT_COMM_MODE, 0, &hIn, &procErr);/* Instantiate service TESTSVC */ uinstopr (hIn, (UCHAR *)"DOLIST", 0, &hOp);/* Instantiate operation DOLIST*/ ulistnew(hEnv, &hList); /* Create an item list */ for ( i=0; countries[i]; i+= 2 ) /* Loop through the data */ { ulistput(hList, UITEM_OPTION_ID, 0, countries[i], countries[i+1] ); /* Add the list item */ } ulistdel(hList, UITEM_OPTION_ID, 0, "?");/* Delete the item with id "?" */ ulist2uf(hOp, 1, hList); /* Copy item list to operation param*/ uopract(hOp, &actStatus, &procErr); /* Activate the operation */ uinstdel (hIn, 0); /* Destroy instance */ ufreeh (&hList); /* Free list handle */ ufreeh (&hOp); /* Free operation handle */ ufreeh (&hIn); /* Free instance handle */ ufreeh (&hEnv); /* Free environment handle */ } Copy The operation DOLIST prints this line to the message frame Explain this code NL=Netherlands;GB=Great Britain;D=Germany;F=France;I=Italy;E=Spain Copy Related Links ulistput Insert an item into an item list. ustr2uf Copy string data from a program buffer to a basic parameter or entity field.
